seoTitle: How to Add Quick Filters to a Jira Board
metaDescription: Learn how to set up quick filters on a Jira Scrum or Kanban board so your team can narrow down issues by assignee, type, or priority in one click.
intro: As a board fills up with issues, finding the ones that matter to you gets harder. Quick filters in Jira let you add one-click toggles that narrow the board down to a specific slice of work, whether that's your own tickets or a particular issue type.

steps.heading: Open your board settings
steps.body: Go to the configuration settings for the Scrum or Kanban board where you want to add a quick filter.

steps.heading: Find the quick filters section
steps.body: Locate the quick filters option within the board's configuration menu.

steps.heading: Create a new filter
steps.body: Add a new quick filter and give it a clear name that describes what it will show, such as 'My Issues' or 'High Priority'.

steps.heading: Write the JQL for the filter
steps.body: Define the filter's logic using Jira Query Language, such as filtering by assignee, issue type, or priority.

steps.heading: Save and test the filter
steps.body: Save your changes, then go to the board and click the new quick filter to confirm it narrows down issues as expected.

faq.question: Do I need to know JQL to create a quick filter?
faq.answer: Yes, quick filters are built using Jira Query Language, though simple filters like filtering by assignee or issue type use fairly basic syntax.

faq.question: Can multiple quick filters be applied at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can typically toggle on more than one quick filter at a time to narrow the board down further.

faq.question: Are quick filters visible to the whole team?
faq.answer: Quick filters are set at the board level, so anyone with access to that board can see and use them.

faq.question: Can I edit a quick filter after creating it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can go back into the board's configuration settings at any time to adjust or remove an existing quick filter.
